prior_scan_disposition.note: All 4 findings from scan-007 addressed: superlative hedged, multi-causal attribution added, CMF citation linked, Braver Angels claim softened.

findings.description: Persuasive reframing maps 'property rights' to ICESCR Article 11 (adequate standard of living). Article 11 provides positive entitlements to food, clothing, and housing — related to but distinct from 'property rights' as conservatives typically use the term (ownership, use, and disposition of property — a negative liberty). The glossary's own ESCR entry acknowledges this positive/negative distinction.
findings.suggestion: Replace 'property rights' with language that accurately describes what Article 11 protects: 'housing security and adequate living standards' or 'economic stability.' Alternatively, add a parenthetical noting the connection rather than asserting equivalence.
findings.context: Conservatives value property rights and family stability — the ICESCR protects both (Articles 10, 11).

findings.description: Voter lens framing states 'Personal letters carry more weight than form letters' as unhedged fact without citation. CMF research supports this claim and appears as a link on line 100, but the voter lens text lacks its own citation or hedging.
findings.suggestion: Add 'research suggests' or link to the CMF citation already present on line 100. Alternatively, use the same hedged phrasing from line 100: 'traditionally carries more weight.'
findings.context: Personal letters carry more weight than form letters.

findings.description: The ADA pattern (domestic legislation, simple majority in both chambers) and ICESCR ratification (international treaty, two-thirds Senate vote) share enforcement-outcome similarities but differ in political mechanics. 'Mirrors' implies closer structural similarity than the evidence supports.
findings.suggestion: Replace 'mirrors' with 'echoes' or 'follows the pattern of' to convey similarity without implying structural equivalence.
findings.context: a composite model...that mirrors how the ADA moved from concept to law.

epistemic_flags: All 3 findings cluster in fair-witness dimension on a single advocacy page — advocacy register inherently tensions with fair witness standards; these represent calibration suggestions, not defects
Finding f1 (property rights mapping) involves interpretive judgment about how conservatives use 'property rights' — reasonable people may disagree on whether the mapping stretches too far
Prior scan findings all addressed — this represents healthy convergence in the feedback loop
